sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Rayo Vallecano, sitting at 9th in the league with 44 points, welcomes Getafe, who are positioned 12th with 39 points, for a crucial Round 34 clash on May 2, 2025, at 19:00. The game will be held at Estadio de Vallecas in Madrid, a venue known for its passionate home support. Referee Victor Garcia Verdura, who averages 4.4 yellow cards and 0.19 red cards per game, will oversee the encounter. This matchup promises to be tightly contested given the proximity of both teams in the standings. With only 5 points separating them, both sides will be eager to secure vital points at this stage of the season.

sections.subtitle: Historical Context
sections.text: Looking at their head-to-head record, Rayo Vallecano holds an edge with no losses in their last 8 meetings against Getafe. In their most recent encounter on August 24, 2024, the game ended in a 0-0 draw, with Getafe’s Luis Milla earning Player of the Match honors with a Sofascore rating of 7.6. Over the last 5 matchups, there has been 1 win for the home team (Rayo Vallecano when playing at home), 4 draws, and no wins for Getafe as the away side. Notably, Rayo Vallecano has kept 3 consecutive clean sheets against Getafe in head-to-head fixtures. Matches between these sides often see under 2.5 goals, with this trend holding in 6 out of their last 6 encounters.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Rayo Vallecano comes into this game without a win in their last 4 matches, though this streak is not currently active. They have also gone without a clean sheet in their last 4 games, but again, this streak has ended. On the other hand, Getafe is on a concerning run of 3 consecutive losses, a streak that remains active. They’ve also failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 3 matches, another ongoing trend. Getafe has been the first to concede in 7 of their last 9 games, which could be a vulnerability Rayo Vallecano might exploit. Both teams have a history of matches with over 2.5 goals in 5 of their last 7 combined games, though this streak isn’t active.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Rayo Vallecano
sections.text: Rayo Vallecano will rely on midfielder Álvaro García, who boasts an expected goals tally of 6.28 and expected assists of 5.13, though he has underperformed his xG by -3.28 with only 3 goals scored. His 5 assists and average rating of 7.03 make him a creative force. Isi Palazón, another midfielder, contributes with 3 goals and 4 assists, holding a solid rating of 6.9. Defender Florian Lejeune, with a rating of 7.04, provides stability at the back alongside 2 assists. However, the absence of forward Sergio Camello, who has 3 goals and 2 assists, could impact their attack.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Getafe
sections.text: Getafe’s standout performer is midfielder Luis Milla, ranked 3rd in the league for key passes with 66, alongside 4 assists and a strong average rating of 7.14. Midfielder Mauro Arambarri leads their scoring charts with 9 goals, overperforming his expected goals of 7.06 by 1.94, and will be a major threat. Defender Omar Alderete, with a rating of 7.06, is a defensive rock and tops the league for percentage of long balls in total passes at 13.8%. Ramón Terrats, in form over the last 5 matches with a rating of 7.6 and 4 goals, will also be crucial. However, they’ll miss defender Diego Rico, who has 3 assists and a rating of 7.03.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Statistics
sections.text: Rayo Vallecano ranks 12th in total goals scored with 36, but they overperform their expected goals of 40.34 by 4.34, placing them 4th in the league for goals versus expected goals difference. They excel in defensive metrics like interceptions per game (8.7, 5th) and clearances per game (23.97, 5th). Getafe, on the other hand, tends to score late, with 10 goals between the 76th and 90th minutes, representing 12.9% of their total. Rayo Vallecano’s possession game is strong at 51.7% (8th in the league), and they create big chances well, ranking 8th with 70 created. Both teams struggle with converting big chances, with Rayo at 28.57% (19th in the league).

sections.subtitle: Disciplinary and Set-Piece Trends
sections.text: Discipline could play a big role in this match, as Rayo Vallecano has committed the 3rd most fouls in the league with 479, accumulating 87 yellow cards (17th fewest). Matches involving referee Victor Garcia Verdura often see cards, averaging 4.4 yellows per game. Head-to-head stats show over 4.5 cards in 6 of the last 7 encounters between these teams. In terms of set-pieces, Rayo Vallecano averages 5.03 corners per game (9th in the league), while historically, games between these teams see under 10.5 corners in 8 of their last 9 meetings.

sections.subtitle: Missing Players Impact
sections.text: Rayo Vallecano will miss key forward Sergio Camello, whose 3 goals and 2 assists could have been vital, alongside defenders Andrei Rațiu and Abdul Mumin, potentially weakening their backline. Getafe also faces challenges with the absence of forward Álvaro Rodriguez, who has 2 goals, and defenders Allan Nyom and Diego Rico, the latter contributing 3 assists. These absences could tilt the balance, especially in defensive solidity and attacking depth for both sides.
